chapter_title.en: The Call to Prayer and the Establishment of Prayer and the Reward of the Muezzins

text: 873 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ اَلسَّرِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «مِنَ اَلسُّنَّةِ إِذَا أَذَّنَ اَلرَّجُلُ أَنْ يَضَعَ إِصْبَعَيْهِ فِي أُذُنَيْهِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.873 - Al-Hasan ibn al-Sari reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"It is part of the Sunnah (tradition) for a person giving the adhan (call to prayer) to place his fingers in his ears."
